title: United States imposes new 12.5% tariffs on Japan and 60 other countries
summary: The new differentiated tariffs replace the universal 10% levy and take effect on July 24.

title: U.S. completes 13 consecutive days of attacks against Iran and Trump threatens a larger offensive
summary: The U.S. president states that Iran 'has not yet suffered enough' while considering a large-scale operation.

title: Kōzō Okamoto, member of Japanese Red Army responsible for the 1972 Lod airport massacre, dies in Lebanon
summary: Okamoto, 78, was internationally wanted for the terrorist attack that left 26 dead in Tel Aviv.

title: Trump visits the Los Angeles Dodgers and praises Shohei Ohtani as 'legend'
summary: The U.S. president attends the game and meets with Japanese players Ohtani, Yamamoto, and Sasaki.

title: Former Japanese volleyball team player admits cannabis possession in first court appearance
summary: Shunichiro Sato, Olympic medalist, pleaded guilty in district court on drug charges.

title: Number of national pension subscribers in Japan falls to record low while payment rate improves
summary: Official data shows a continuous decline in contributors, although the premium payment rate reaches a record.

title: Japan's consumer inflation rises 1.6% in June, accelerating for first time in three months
summary: The consumer price index exceeded 1.5% but remains below the Bank of Japan's 2% target.

title: Japan enacts reform of Imperial House Law allowing women to retain royal status after marriage
summary: The law, which takes effect in October, also allows adoption of members from collateral branches to ensure succession.

title: Iyogin Holdings and Ehime Bank agree to merge to create a bank with about 10 trillion yen in deposits
summary: The integration, scheduled for next April, will create one of the largest regional financial institutions in Japan.

title: Russian fifth-generation Su-57 fighter jet crashes near Moscow during training flight
summary: The pilot managed to eject and survived, according to Russian sources, in the first known crash of this advanced model.

title: Intelligence unit of Japan's Ground Forces illegally spied on thousands of citizens
summary: The unit collected private information on political and religious opinions without authorization, according to reports.

title: Takeshi Oiwa to become new coach of Japan's national soccer team from 2026
summary: The Japan Football Association appoints the former under-23 coach amid criticism over the lack of transparency in the selection process.

title: Opposition parties in Japan progress in negotiations for possible merger or electoral alliance
summary: Secretaries-general of the Constitutional Democratic Party, Innovation Party, and Komeito agree to present an interim report.

title: Former Japan Finance Corporation employee arrested for alleged bribery with luxury watches in exchange for loans
summary: The former official allegedly facilitated loans in exchange for valuable watches, in a corruption case that tarnishes the public entity.

title: Rintaro Sasaki signs with Miami Marlins and speaks fluent English at press conference
summary: The young Japanese slugger, a former student of the prestigious Hanamaki Higashi High School, follows in the footsteps of Ichiro Suzuki.

title: Trump conditions civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia on normalization of relations with Israel
summary: The U.S. president links atomic cooperation with Riyadh to recognition of the Jewish state, amid Middle East negotiations.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 1,500 points dragged down by tech slump on Wall Street
summary: The Japanese market suffers its biggest drop in weeks, losing the 65,000 yen level, on fears of a global slowdown.

title: Use of generative AI in Japan focuses on work, while in the US it is used as a 'friend'
summary: Only 60% of Japanese use generative AI compared to 70% in the US, where personal and recreational use predominates.

title: Japan establishes National Intelligence Agency and appoints Kazuya Hara as its first director
summary: The new agency, which will begin operations on July 31, will centralize the country's intelligence functions to improve security.

title: Yokohama defeats Keio in national high school baseball tournament with record 157 km/h pitch
summary: Pitcher Shoki Oda recorded the fastest speed of his career in the high-profile summer championship match.

title: Ryuji Kawahara takes office as new Prosecutor-General of Japan, replacing the first woman in the position
summary: Kawahara, previously chief prosecutor of Tokyo, succeeds Mitsuru Unemoto as the top authority of the Public Prosecutors Office.
